recent_calls_14d.subject: Mair Group/ Zycus project
recent_calls_14d.snippet:
Babe, I think this should be, in short, sweet and simple, quick discussion. First of all, thank you both Mohamad and Karl for joining us on this discussion, and thank you, Mohamad, also for arranging the discussion. Karl, as per the e mail that I had sent you regarding the invoice, we just had a concern regarding the timing of the invoice. We had a quick discussion with Mohamad on Monday for the kickoff for the implementation of this, and like I had highlighted or alluded to in my e mail to you, Karl, is that typically in most of our implementations, and this is something that, unfortunately, I didn't capture or communicate to my team. But in previous implementations, usually, we don't get invoiced or at least we arrange with our service providers that the invoicing will kick off when we, as the company, are starting to actually play with the environment where there's a license required, which is typically in the UAT stage.
So this was something that I had expressed to to Mohamad that currently, this solution that we're putting in place is due to us restructuring the department, and we wanna make sure that we're doing something that's done correctly. But we wanted to see what. Zycus could do to accommodate this. So I told them we acknowledge so if I could we acknowledge that the licensing will be paid upfront full for the whole year. That's typical. That's nothing odd. But again, we're just looking at it from a best practice perspective. Go ahead, Carl.
1: Yeah. No worries. T

recent_calls_14d.subject: SOW Workshop - See Agenda & Required Attendees Below

our_open_deliverables.text: Issue the licence invoice with the agreed 60-day payment terms
Rewrite the payment schedule splitting design sign-off into phased milestones

buyer_dependent_items.text: Confirm acceptance of the phased-invoicing / 60-day payment compromise
Provide SOW workshop dates and schedule the sponsor kickoff

competitive_position.summary: No active competitor as of 3 Jul 2026: the deal is signed and executing. GEP Worldwide was the only live peer (50% below Zycus pricing per 3 Apr 2026 note) and is OUT since Zycus was selected 3 Apr and signed 17 Jun. Coupa (abandoned as overcomplicated) and SAP Ariba (licences written off) are historical; Ivalua, JAGGAER and Oracle are awareness-set names never raised on calls; Kissflow was a build-vs-buy alternative resolved in Zycus's favour. The only residual threat is a GEP re-approach if Phase 1 delivery stumbles.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.quote: Follow up call with the champion afterwards confirmed informally we are the preferred vendor and are in competition with GEP. Note GEP are 50% of our pricing in the Al Tasnim opp.
competitors.how_we_win: Speed-to-value via pre-built SAP B1/S4HANA/Yardi connectors vs GEP's custom build; unified three-ERP experience; regional Middle East team; SaaS-fit contracting. Protect the win by keeping Phase 1 green so GEP has no re-entry.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.how_we_win: Mair already tried and abandoned Coupa as overcomplicated; Zycus positioned as simpler and fit for the three-ERP environment.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Ariba licences acquired but written off.
competitors.how_we_win: Ariba is unused inventory; Mair moved on before Zycus engaged.

competitors.name: Ivalua
competitors.source: SF Competitors__c field only; never raised on calls or Next Step
competitors.how_we_win: Awareness-set name only; no active evaluation.

competitors.name: JAGGAER
competitors.source: SF Competitors__c field only; never raised on calls or Next Step
competitors.how_we_win: Awareness-set name only; no active evaluation.

competitors.name: Oracle
competitors.source: SF Competitors__c field only; never raised on calls or Next Step
competitors.how_we_win: Awareness-set name only; no active evaluation.

competitors.name: Kissflow (BPM build-vs-buy)
competitors.quote: we are going to get rid of Kisflow ... just to integrate with SAP, it took us 7 weeks
competitors.how_we_win: Buy-over-build already chosen; Zycus pre-built connectors beat custom integration effort.

champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole (Vishal Kapil, Lovemer Raymundo) + Avoma calls Nov 2025-Jun 2026
champion_strength.summary: The deal closed on strong economic sponsorship rather than a classic coaching champion. Vishal Kapil (Group CIO & CTO, Decision Maker) is a credible technical validator who joined every major session but is a validator, not an internal seller; Mazin (Director of Procurement) drove the deal to signature as the economic buyer, not as a coach. Lovemer Raymundo, the most frequent Mair contact and likely Phase 1 project lead, is the best candidate to build into an active champion by giving him early Phase 1 wins. At this stage (signed, executing) the absence of a coaching champion is a Phase-1 execution watch-item, not a deal risk.
champion_strength.champion: Vishal Kapil (Group CIO & CTO, Decision Maker) is the closest technical champion; Lovemer Raymundo is the operational lead to develop into an advocate.

sources: OpportunityContactRole (Mazin Abdulhadi Hawasli, Director of Procurement)
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Mazin Abdulhadi Hawasli (Director of Procurement) is the economic buyer and commercial authority: he opened the 21 Apr F2F by promising to pull out 'a big baseball bat and asking you to give me a nice little reduction in price. About 49%', drove the entire pricing negotiation, and countersigned the agreement on Mair's behalf on 12 Jun (per DocuSign process agreed on the 10 Jun legal call). Above him sits an SVP who signed off the commercials, referenced by Dan on the 6 May call ('get Mazin to talk to the senior vice president who signed all this off'), the ultimate budget owner. Mazin is fully engaged and bought-in (last contact 29 Jun email). His authority showed again in the invoice dispute where his team, not Zycus, set the payment-timing terms.

recommended_moves: Confirm the 60-day phased-invoicing compromise in writing with Mazin to unblock Phase 1.
Lock the SOW workshop output into a signed SOW to trigger delivery kickoff.
Complete and return the signed NDA and finish customer/billing onboarding.
